title: Processes, Signals, Jobs, and Resource Control · Guided Terminal Studio
summary: Diagnose a worker that appears hung while preserving evidence, avoiding collateral termination, and distinguishing shell jobs from system processes. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a process-tree incident and controlled-recovery runbook while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.
Use ps, pgrep, pidof, top-like evidence, and proc data with filters that identify the intended process uniquely.
Send signals from least disruptive to forceful while accounting for handlers, permissions, descendants, and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Process tree has shell 2100, worker 2201, and child 2207; the worker stops responding but owns a temporary report. Identify the intended process, capture state, send the least disruptive signal, and verify cleanup.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for relate executable files, processes, threads, parentage, sessions, process groups, state, and open resources.

content.title: Evidence defense
